column1: 3 endpoint(s). Mock definitions — assume realistic data shapes.
List published entries via fast CDN. Equivalent to WP /posts. Most-used Contentful read endpoint.

column5: Bearer token using the Content Delivery API access token (read-only, scoped to a space).
Which Contentful space (a workspace) and environment (master | staging | preview) to read from.

column4: Field name with optional '-' prefix e.g. '-sys.updatedAt'
column5: Sort by field, ascending or descending

column4: Filter on system fields (id, createdAt, updatedAt, etc.)
Locale code e.g. 'en-US'. Use 'all' for all locales
column5: Which language version of the content to return. Use '*' to get all locales for each entry.

column4: Resolve linked references depth (saves round trips)
column5: Auto-resolve linked entries/assets up to N levels deep
Comma-separated list of fields to return — lets you slim down the payload when you only need a few attributes.
Pagination state echoed back — what offset and page size the response was built with. Useful for the next paged call.
Lifecycle timestamps — when the entry was created, last edited, and last published. The gap between updated and published tells you if changes are pending.
Version (every save, including drafts) and revision (published versions only). Comparing them tells you if a draft exists ahead of what's live.
Which locale this particular field set is in. Relevant when the entry has multiple language versions.

column4: Field values per content type schema. Reference fields contain {sys: {type:'Link', linkType:'Entry|Asset', id}}. RichText fields contain document AST
column5: The actual content fields the editor filled in — shape depends on the content model for this entry type.
Linked entries returned inline so you don't need a second call (e.g., the author entry referenced by a post).

column1: Get single entry by ID. Faster than list when you have the ID.

column5: Which space, environment, and specific entry to fetch.

column1: Single entry object — same shape as items[] in list
column5: Returns one entry with the same fields/sys/metadata shape as a single row from the list endpoint.

column1: Metadata tags for cross-content-type taxonomy. Available via both CDA (read) and CMA (write).

column5: To create a tag, supply a display name, a slug (sys.id — also used in URLs), and whether it's visible publicly or only internally.
Each tag's metadata — slug, resource type marker, visibility, when it was created and last updated, and current version number.
Display label shown to editors and (if public) to end users.
